title: These 5 Risk Rules Save Failing Traders
description: Most traders focus on entries but ignore risk management. Here are the 5 essential risk management rules that separate profitable traders from the 90% who fail. Master these rules before your next trade! Follow @R2FTrading for more ICT trading education and risk management strategies.

scenes.text: Most traders fail because of this. They focus on entries, not exits.
scenes.visualQuery: frustrated trader looking at computer screen with hands on head

scenes.text: Rule one. Never risk more than two percent per trade.
scenes.visualQuery: trading position sizing calculator showing 2% risk management on EUR/USD chart

scenes.text: Rule two. Always set your stop loss before entry.
scenes.visualQuery: GBP/USD chart showing stop loss placement below support level with entry marked

scenes.text: Rule three. Risk to reward must be one to two minimum.
scenes.visualQuery: USD/JPY chart displaying 1:2 risk reward ratio with measured entry, stop loss and take profit levels

scenes.text: Rule four. Never move stops against you. Rule five. Size down after losses.
scenes.visualQuery: AUD/USD chart showing proper stop loss management with original stop level maintained

scenes.text: Follow for more trading rules. Your account will thank you.
scenes.visualQuery: confident trader smiling while looking at profitable trading results on screen

script: Most traders fail because of this. They focus on entries, not exits. Rule one. Never risk more than two percent per trade. Rule two. Always set your stop loss before entry. Rule three. Risk to reward must be one to two minimum. Rule four. Never move stops against you. Rule five. Size down after losses. Follow for more trading rules. Your account will thank you.
